corpora.description: 761 production-merged Prisma-style Postgres migrations from three established open-source repositories. First calibration corpus used for verify's migration vertical. Canonical corpus for DM-18.

sources.name: cal.com
sources.notes: Largest slice of the corpus. Prisma-generated Postgres migrations with long history going back to 2021.

sources.name: supabase
sources.notes: Hand-written Supabase migration examples from the supabase repo. Contains native Postgres migrations across multiple example projects.

suitability.suitability_reason: Production-merged filters out grounding-shape failures (DM-01..05) because migrations that reference non-existent tables or columns would have failed CI or code review and never been merged. Prisma's defensive migration generator pre-empts safety-shape failures (DM-15, DM-16, DM-17, DM-19) by emitting constraint drops before column or table drops, by computing type changes through intermediate columns rather than direct narrowings, and by managing indexes through named constraint definitions. DM-18 is the exception because it fires on a runtime data failure (NOT NULL without DEFAULT against a non-empty table) that no generator can pre-empt and no code review can catch without knowing row counts.

reproducibility.notes: The corpus is reproduced by running backtest.ts to clone the three repos, then replay-engine.ts to run the grounding and safety gates against each migration in sequence. Snapshot SHAs for each repo's state at the time of the original calibration are not currently pinned; re-running the clone will pull the latest main branch of each repo, which may differ from the April 2026 snapshot.

limitations: Prisma-heavy: 730 of 761 files are Prisma-generated. Results on hand-written SQL, Django, Rails, or Alembic migrations are not measured.
Production-merged: the corpus contains only migrations that passed CI and code review, which structurally filters out many failure modes that exist in pre-merge or draft states.
Single framework family: all three repos use Prisma or hand-written Supabase migrations. The corpus does not exercise migration generators from other ecosystems.
Commit SHAs for the original April 2026 snapshot are not pinned in this record and the clone reproduces against current main, which will drift over time.

candidates.description: Planned second corpus. Django migrations from 3-5 mid-sized open-source projects (Sentry, Mailman, Read the Docs, Saleor, Misago candidates). Target: 200-500 migrations with less defensive generator pre-emption than Prisma, enabling DM-15, DM-16, and DM-17 calibration attempts under the bar.
candidates.spec_reference: scripts/mvp-migration/MEASURED-CLAIMS.md section 'Calibration corpus #2 — spec for the next calibration source'
candidates.status_notes: Not yet sourced. Listed as candidate so the registry reflects the documented roadmap.
